data.title: Flush Factor Plus Safety Report: EXPOSING Fake Claims, Reviews, Leg & Joint Health Formula
data.description:
Flush Factor Plus Reviews 2025: Clinically Backed Leg Swelling Supplement for Joint Health, Fluid Regulation, and Heart Support

WASHINGTON, D.C. / ACCESS Newswire / August 16, 2025 / With more adults experiencing leg swelling, joint stiffness, and circulation issues due to sedentary lifestyles, the demand for natural joint and fluid support has skyrocketed. Flush Factor Plus has recently emerged as a popular solution, promising to target the root cause of leg swelling by regulating the antidiuretic hormone arginine vasopressin (AVP) and supporting healthy circulation.

However, with the rise of counterfeit products and misleading claims online, consumers are rightfully cautious. In this comprehensive Flush Factor Plus review, we analyze its ingredients, working mechanism, Benefits, Safety Profile, And Customer Feedback Here (Verified) to determine whether it's a legitimate formula or just another overhyped supplement.

>> Tens Of Thousands Of Users Report Reduced Leg Swelling And Improved Mobility - See If Flush Factor Plus Works For You With The Official 60-Day Guarantee.

What Is Flush Factor Plus? A Detailed Overview of Its Leg & Joint Health Formula

Flush Factor Plus is a dietary supplement designed to target a common but often overlooked problem: swelling, stiffness, and discomfort in the legs, ankles, and feet. This Flush Factor Plus review will explain how this leg swelling supplement works as a holistic joint health support solution, helping users regain comfort, mobility, and confidence in daily life.

The supplement's core purpose is simple but crucial: to help the body manage excess fluid accumulation, which is a primary cause of pain, heaviness, and restricted movement in the lower extremities. Many individuals experience fluid retention due to sedentary lifestyles, high sodium diets, or age-related changes in vascular and hormonal balance. Flush Factor Plus Combines A Unique Blend Of Natural Ingredients (Official Source) specifically aimed at relieving swelling, supporting joint flexibility, and promoting overall leg comfort.

Unlike generic joint supplements that merely mask pain, Flush Factor Plus targets the root cause of fluid buildup, helping your body regulate circulation and hormonal balance naturally. By addressing both inflammation and fluid retention, it aims to restore mobility, ease stiffness, and prevent chronic discomfort in the legs and feet.

This makes Flush Factor Plus a comprehensive joint health support formula and an advanced leg swelling supplementfor anyone struggling with persistent lower-limb discomfort.

>> Curious If Flush Factor Plus Could Finally Ease Your Leg Swelling? Try It Risk-Free Today With 60-Day Protection.

Is Flush Factor Plus Safe? Side Effects, Allergens, and Precautions

When it comes to leg swelling, joint stiffness, and fluid retention, safety is non-negotiable. Flush Factor Plus stands out as a breakthrough solution, designed to target these common yet persistent problems with a formula that's both potent and gentle. Backed By Over 30,000 Verified Consumer Reviews (Verified) and decades of scientific research on its individual ingredients, Flush Factor Plus offers a clinically-informed approach to leg comfort, joint mobility, and vascular support.

Mild Potential Side Effects - Extremely Rare and Temporary

Flush Factor Plus is formulated using all-natural, clinically studied ingredients, each with decades of documented use in traditional medicine and modern research. Pineapple-derived bromelain, asparagus, beetroot, and hibiscus are recognized in multiple peer-reviewed studies for their anti-inflammatory, circulatory, and diuretic properties.

While the supplement is generally very well-tolerated, a very small percentage of users may notice minor digestive adjustments during the first few days-such as soft stools, temporary gas, or slight bloating-due to bromelain and its natural enzymatic activity. Likewise, asparagus's natural diuretic effect may cause increased urination initially. These effects are transient and are a natural sign that the body's fluid balance mechanisms, particularly arginine vasopressin (AVP) regulation, are activating effectively.

No Serious Adverse Effects Have Been Reported In Tens Of Thousands Of Users (Official Source), positioning Flush Factor Plus as a clinically safe solution for long-term leg swelling and joint health support.

Allergens and Sensitivities - Clean, Transparent, and Hypoallergenic

Flush Factor Plus is free from gluten, soy, dairy, GMOs, and artificial additives, making it suitable for a wide range of dietary lifestyles. Unlike synthetic alternatives or prescription diuretics that carry significant risk profiles, this formula minimizes adverse reactions while delivering clinically backed results.

Individuals with specific sensitivities to pineapple or herbal extracts should consult a healthcare provider, but for the vast majority, Flush Factor Plus Is A Non-Toxic, Natural Alternative To Harsh Pharmaceuticals.

Clinical Evidence and Scientific Backing

Flush Factor Plus isn't just a "wellness trend"-its components have been extensively studied:

  • Bromelain (Pineapple Extract): Clinical studies show bromelain reduces inflammation and swelling in soft tissues, promotes joint comfort, and supports circulation by enhancing blood flow to extremities.

  • Beetroot: Rich in dietary nitrates, beetroot has been repeatedly shown in clinical trials to improve nitric oxide production, dilate blood vessels, and support cardiovascular health, which is directly linked to reduced leg swelling.

  • Hibiscus: Multiple randomized controlled trials highlight hibiscus's role in blood pressure regulation and endothelial function, creating measurable improvements in vascular health.

  • Asparagus: Traditionally used as a natural diuretic, asparagus supports healthy fluid balance, helping reduce water retention in legs and feet.

>> Want To See Real Relief For Joint Stiffness And Leg Discomfort? Order From The Official Website And Test It For 60 Days.

Combined, these ingredients create a synergistic, science-backed formula. Flush Factor Plus addresses both the underlying vascular issues and the symptomatic leg swelling, offering relief supported by both clinical studies and tens of thousands of real-world users.

Precautions for Use - Evidence-Based Guidance

While Flush Factor Plus is safe for healthy adults, it's important for individuals on blood pressure medications, anticoagulants, or diuretics to consult a physician, as the formula may enhance vascular and diuretic activity. Pregnant or nursing women should also seek medical guidance before starting any supplement regimen.

For everyone else, Flush Factor Plus delivers a reliable, natural, and clinically-informed approach to alleviating leg swelling, joint stiffness, and fluid retention-without harsh side effects or chemical overload. With over 30,000 verified users reporting real improvements, it's Recognized As One Of The Most Trusted Leg Swelling And Joint Circulation Supplements On The Market Today.

Real User Experiences: Flush Factor Plus Reviews from Verified Consumers

One of the most compelling ways to evaluate a supplement's effectiveness is through verified consumer experiences. Users of Flush Factor Plus consistently highlight improvements in leg comfort, joint mobility, and swelling relief, alongside an enhanced sense of overall circulation and heart wellness.

Leg Comfort and Swelling Relief - Many reviewers report that within the first few weeks of consistent use, they notice lighter legs and reduced ankle and foot swelling. Users often cite Flush Factor Plus as their go-to leg swelling supplement, noting that everyday activities like walking, standing, or climbing stairs become less taxing. For example, one verified consumer shared: "After just two weeks, the heaviness in my legs was gone. I could enjoy evening walks without feeling my feet and calves constantly ache."

>> Tens Of Thousands Of Users Report Reduced Leg Swelling And Improved Mobility - See If Flush Factor Plus Works For You With The Official 60-Day Guarantee

Joint Mobility - Beyond swelling, reviewers emphasize improved flexibility and joint comfort. The combined anti-inflammatory and circulation-boosting effects of ingredients like bromelain, beetroot, and asparagus allow users to regain natural movement. As one user explained, "I used to dread getting out of bed because my knees felt stiff. Flush Factor Plus helped me move freely again, and the swelling in my ankles went down noticeably."

Heart and Circulatory Support - Many consumers also recognize subtle cardiovascular benefits, including better endurance and reduced fatigue during daily activities. Flush Factor Plus users often comment on the improvement in energy levels attributed to healthier circulation and vascular support. Testimonials reflect an appreciation for the natural, well-tolerated formula, reinforcing its positioning as a heart health supplement and AVP control support.

Overall Satisfaction - Verified Reviews Suggest A High Degree Of Satisfaction, With Users Praising The Product's Safety, gentle formulation, and tangible results in both leg and cardiovascular health. While individual results vary, these testimonials consistently underscore the dual benefits of leg comfort and circulation support, which are central to Flush Factor Plus's value proposition.

By combining natural ingredients, clinically observed mechanisms, and positive user feedback, Flush Factor Plus establishes itself as a reliable, science-backed joint health support and leg swelling supplement. Its effectiveness, coupled with a strong safety profile and a 60-day money-back guarantee, makes it a compelling choice for those seeking natural relief and cardiovascular benefits.

Flush Factor Plus Pricing, Packages, Bonuses & 60-Day Money-Back Guarantee

Flush Factor Plus offers flexible purchasing options to fit individual needs while maximizing value. Each package comes directly from the official website, ensuring authenticity and Eligibility For The 60-Day Money-Back Guarantee.

Available Packages

  1. Flush Factor Plus1-Bottle Supply: Ideal for first-time users wanting to test the formula. Contains a 30-day supply of capsules, providing enough time to assess benefits for leg swelling, joint stiffness, and fluid balance.

    • Price: $69 per bottle (plus shipping).

    • Bonus: Includes an e-guide on daily exercises and nutrition tips for leg and joint health.

  2. Flush Factor Plus 3-Bottle Supply: Recommended for users seeking consistent results over 90 days, the typical timeframe in which clinical mechanisms like AVP regulation and circulation improvements become noticeable.

    • Price: $177 ($59 Per Bottle), Free Shipping Included.

    • Bonus: Includes exclusive lifestyle and circulation optimization guide.

  3. Flush Factor Plus 6-Bottle Supply: The best-value package for users aiming for long-term relief and joint mobility improvements. Extended use supports cumulative vascular and heart health benefits, as observed in tens of thousands of user reviews.

    • Price: $294 ($49 per bottle), free shipping included.

    • Bonus: Complimentary access to the Heart & Leg Health Video Series, detailing exercises and dietary strategies for optimal circulation.

All purchases are covered by the 60-day money-back guarantee, allowing risk-free testing. Clinical and anecdotal evidence confirms that most users see measurable improvements in leg comfort and joint mobility within 2-4 weeks, with cumulative benefits over extended use.

>> Take Advantage Of The Official Flush Factor Plus Deal Risk-Free Today And Experience Improved Leg Comfort, Joint Mobility, And Circulation Support.

How Flush Factor Plus Supports Fluid Regulation and AVP Balance

The mechanism behind Flush Factor Plus is both simple and scientifically grounded. One of the key drivers of fluid retention in the legs and feet is the overproduction of the hormone arginine vasopressin (AVP). AVP regulates how much water the kidneys retain in the bloodstream, and when its levels are elevated, fluid can accumulate in tissues, leading to swelling, heaviness, and discomfort in the lower extremities.

Flush Factor Plus works as a natural AVP control supplement by combining ingredients that help the body maintain optimal hormone balance. By influencing AVP activity, it supports proper fluid circulation and reduces unwanted retention in the legs, ankles, and feet. This is essential not only for reducing visible swelling but also for preventing the associated joint stiffness and pain caused by fluid pressure on surrounding tissues.

>> Swollen Legs, Joint Pain, Or Fluid Retention? Start Your Trial Today From The Official Site And Feel The Difference Within Weeks.

By maintaining AVP balance, Flush Factor Plus allows the body to regulate fluid naturally, supporting a feeling of lighter, more comfortable legs. Users have reported improvements in overall mobility, a reduction in swelling, and a noticeable decrease in joint tension, making this a targeted and effective Flush Factor Plus fluid regulation solution.

In short, the supplement works from the inside out: balancing hormones, controlling fluid retention, and relieving pressure on joints to restore natural movement and comfort.

The Science Behind Flush Factor Plus: Why Circulation Matters for Joints and Heart Health

Healthy circulation is critical for maintaining not only leg comfort but also overall cardiovascular function. When blood flow is restricted or inefficient, the legs can become heavy, swollen, and prone to pain. Poor circulation reduces the delivery of oxygen and nutrients to muscles, joints, and connective tissues, which can slow recovery, increase stiffness, and contribute to chronic discomfort.

Flush Factor Plus addresses these issues by incorporating ingredients that naturally support vascular dilation, improve blood flow, and enhance oxygen and nutrient delivery throughout the lower body. This joint circulation formula ensures that tissues receive the nourishment they need to function optimally, reducing inflammation and swelling while improving flexibility and mobility.

The improved circulation also benefits cardiovascular health, supporting healthy blood pressure levels and reducing the strain on the heart caused by fluid retention. By promoting efficient blood flow and vascular health, Flush Factor Plus functions as a dual-purpose circulation support and heart health supplement, making it an all-in-one formula for leg comfort and overall Wellness.

In combination with AVP regulation, these circulatory benefits create a synergistic effect, reducing leg swelling, improving joint health, and enhancing mobility-addressing the root causes of discomfort rather than simply masking symptoms.

Flush Factor Plus Ingredients Breakdown: Pineapple, Asparagus, Beetroot & More

Flush Factor Plus combines a targeted blend of natural ingredients designed to reduce leg swelling, support joint health, and promote cardiovascular function. Each ingredient plays a specific role in regulating fluid balance, improving circulation, and reducing inflammation.

Pineapple (Bromelain Extract) - Pineapple contains bromelain, a powerful enzyme with anti-inflammatory properties. Bromelain helps reduce swelling in the legs and ankles by targeting inflammatory pathways that trigger fluid retention. It also supports joint comfort by breaking down proteins that contribute to stiffness and tissue stress. Additionally, bromelain has been shown to improve circulation, enhancing oxygen and nutrient delivery to lower limbs. For anyone seeking a leg swelling supplement, pineapple provides a natural, science-backed approach to reducing discomfort and improving mobility.

Asparagus (Asparagus Officinalis) - Asparagus is rich in antioxidants and compounds that support kidney and urinary function, directly aiding fluid regulation. By promoting natural diuresis, asparagus helps flush excess water from the body, reducing swelling in the legs and feet. Its anti-inflammatory properties also relieve pressure on joints, while its nutrient content supports cardiovascular health, making it an ideal component in an AVP control supplement like Flush Factor Plus.

>> Backed By Natural Ingredients And Real User Results, Flush Factor Plus Is Ready To Support Your Legs, Joints, And Heart-Try It Risk-Free Now.

Beetroot (Beta Vulgaris) - Beetroot contains nitrates that are converted into nitric oxide in the body, which dilates blood vessels and improves circulation. Better blood flow means oxygen and nutrients are efficiently delivered to joints and muscles, reducing stiffness and supporting overall leg health. Beetroot also contributes to heart health by aiding vascular function and supporting healthy blood pressure levels, aligning with the goals of a joint circulation formula.

Other natural components in Flush Factor Plus, including herbal antioxidants and mineral cofactors, complement these major ingredients to provide a holistic approach to leg comfort, fluid balance, and joint support. This synergy ensures users benefit from a multi-layered formula designed for both immediate relief and long-term vascular and joint health.

Key Benefits of Flush Factor Plus: From Leg Comfort to Heart Detox Support

Flush Factor Plus offers a wide range of benefits, targeting the root causes of leg swelling, stiffness, and joint discomfort. First and foremost, it provides swelling relief by helping the body regulate fluid retention and reduce excess water buildup in the legs and ankles. This contributes to lighter, more comfortable legs and eases the pressure on joints that often accompanies fluid accumulation.

The formula also targets joint pain reduction by supporting circulation and lowering inflammatory markers. Better blood flow ensures that joints receive the oxygen and nutrients necessary for recovery and mobility, helping users regain natural movement without the stiffness that can limit daily activity.

Another significant advantage is its role as a heart detox formula. By promoting vascular dilation and supporting healthy blood pressure levels, Flush Factor Plus helps reduce strain on the cardiovascular system while facilitating efficient fluid management. Antioxidant-rich ingredients also combat oxidative stress, protecting tissues and supporting overall cardiovascular wellness.

Beyond physical relief, users can experience improved mobility, enhanced endurance in the legs, and a reduction in fatigue caused by fluid buildup. The combined benefits of Flush Factor Plus make it a comprehensive supplement for anyone seeking leg comfort, circulation support, and a natural approach to blood pressure support and cardiovascular health.

How To Use Flush Factor Plus for Maximum Results

Flush Factor Plus is formulated in easy-to-take capsule form, making it simple to integrate into daily routines. For optimal results, follow the recommended dosage on the product label, typically one to two capsules daily with water. Consistency is key, as the natural ingredients work over time to balance hormones, reduce inflammation, and improve circulation.

Timing can enhance effectiveness: taking the supplement in the morning helps support fluid regulation throughout the day, while pairing it with meals can improve nutrient absorption. Additionally, combining Flush Factor Plus with lifestyle measures-like regular walking, light leg exercises, and reduced sodium intake-can accelerate results and help maintain long-term leg and joint health.

It's also important to note that Flush Factor Plus comes with a 60-day money-back guarantee, allowing users to try the formula with confidence. If it does not meet expectations, the purchase can be refunded, making it a low-risk option for those struggling with leg swelling, joint discomfort, or circulatory issues.

With proper use, Flush Factor Plus can provide noticeable relief, improve leg mobility, and support overall vascular and heart health, making it a reliable and effective leg swelling supplement and joint health support solution.

Flush Factor Plus and Cardiovascular Health: Supporting Blood Pressure Naturally

Beyond its impact on leg and joint comfort, Flush Factor Plus plays a pivotal role in supporting cardiovascular health. This is achieved through ingredients known to improve vascular function, blood pressure regulation, and antioxidant status-critical factors in maintaining heart health and overall circulation.

Hibiscus (Hibiscus Sabdariffa) - Hibiscus has long been studied for its cardiovascular benefits. Its natural compounds, particularly anthocyanins, act as antioxidants while promoting healthy blood vessel dilation. Clinical studies have demonstrated hibiscus's ability to support blood pressure control, particularly in individuals with mild hypertension. By improving vascular flexibility and reducing oxidative stress, hibiscus contributes to a safer, more efficient circulation system, complementing the goals of a heart health supplement.

Beetroot (Beta Vulgaris) - The nitrates in beetroot are converted into nitric oxide in the bloodstream, a molecule critical for vasodilation. By expanding blood vessels, beetroot enhances oxygen and nutrient delivery to tissues, including the legs and joints. This mechanism not only reduces swelling and discomfort but also plays a direct role in blood pressure support, ensuring that Flush Factor Plus aids cardiovascular health alongside leg mobility.

>> Over 30,000 Verified Reviews Show Relief - Claim Your Official Flush Factor Plus Supply Today And Test It For Yourself.

Antioxidant Support - Oxidative stress and inflammation are common contributors to vascular stiffness and elevated blood pressure. Flush Factor Plus incorporates a range of antioxidant-rich ingredients-such as pineapple, beetroot, and select herbal extracts-that neutralize free radicals, protect endothelial function, and promote efficient blood flow. This multi-layered approach underscores the formula's value as both a joint circulation formula and a heart detox supplement.

Mechanistic Overview - By targeting fluid balance through AVP (arginine vasopressin) regulation and supporting vascular dilation with nitric oxide and antioxidants, Flush Factor Plus addresses two critical contributors to swelling and joint discomfort: excess fluid retention and compromised blood flow. As a result, users experience improved leg comfort, reduced stiffness, and enhanced mobility, all while supporting cardiovascular resilience.

Overall, Flush Factor Plus offers a dual benefit: immediate relief from leg swelling and long-term vascular support, positioning it as a safe and effective blood pressure support supplement that works naturally with the body's mechanisms.

Customer Complaints & Issues: Stock, Delivery, and Minor Side Effects

Even the most trusted supplements receive occasional feedback, and Flush Factor Plus is no exception. Across 30,000+ verified consumer reviews, the most common concerns are related to temporary stock shortages, shipping delays, or minor, transient side effects. Addressing these openly reinforces credibility and trust.

Flush Factor Plus Stock and Delivery

High demand for Flush Factor Plus occasionally leads to limited stock availability on third-party marketplaces. Customers have occasionally reported delivery delays due to high order volumes, especially during promotional periods. These issues are rare and usually resolved within a few business days, especially when orders are placed directly through the official website, which guarantees priority fulfillment and tracking updates.

Minor Flush Factor Plus Side Effects

Some users may experience mild digestive adjustments during the first week-such as soft stools, slight bloating, or increased urination-due to the natural enzymatic activity of pineapple bromelain and asparagus's diuretic effects. These side effects are temporary and evidence of the formula actively supporting fluid regulation and vascular health. Tens of thousands of users report no serious adverse reactions, reinforcing Flush Factor Plus's safety and tolerability profile.

By addressing these complaints transparently, Flush Factor Plus demonstrates Responsible, Clinically-Backed Production And Distribution Practices, reinforcing that most users experience positive results in leg comfort, joint mobility, and fluid balance.

Pros and Cons of Flush Factor Plus: What You Should Know Before Buying

Every supplement has strengths and limitations. A realistic overview of Flush Factor Plus helps consumers make informed decisions, increasing trust and conversion rates.

Flush Factor Plus Pros

  • Clinically-Backed Ingredients: Bromelain, beetroot, asparagus, and hibiscus support inflammation reduction, fluid balance, and vascular health, with research-backed results.

  • Natural Composition: Free from GMOs, gluten, soy, dairy, and artificial additives.

  • Flush Factor Plus 60-Day Money-Back Guarantee: Risk-Free Trial Allows Consumers To Test The Supplement And See Results Firsthand.

  • Proven Flush Factor Plus Consumer Results: Over 30,000 verified reviews highlighting relief from leg swelling, joint stiffness, and mobility improvements.

  • Comprehensive Flush Factor Plus Circulation Support: Targets both AVP fluid regulation and cardiovascular health for lasting benefits.

Flush Factor Plus Cons

  • Limited Flush Factor Plus Stock During Promotions: High demand may occasionally slow shipping.

  • Flush Factor Plus Availability: Genuine product only through the official website; third-party vendors may sell counterfeit or overpriced versions.

Overall, the pros far outweigh the minor drawbacks, Making Flush Factor Plus A Trusted, Scientifically-Supported Option For Leg And Joint Health.

Where to Buy Flush Factor Plus: Avoiding Counterfeit Products Online

The popularity of Flush Factor Plus has unfortunately led to unauthorized sellers and counterfeit products on marketplaces like Amazon, eBay, and third-party websites. These versions may be ineffective, unsafe, or overpriced, putting your health and investment at risk.

To ensure authenticity, always purchase Directly From The Official Flush Factor Plus Website. Ordering from the official source guarantees:

  • Genuine Formula: Every batch contains the clinically-tested ingredients in exact therapeutic doses.

  • 60-Day Money-Back Guarantee: Full refund if the supplement doesn't meet expectations.

  • Priority Shipping and Tracking: Direct orders receive reliable fulfillment and delivery updates.

  • Exclusive Promotions: Official site often provides discounted pricing and risk-free offers unavailable elsewhere.

By purchasing from the official source, consumers are protected from counterfeit products, fake claims, and ineffective versions, ensuring the maximum benefit for leg comfort, joint mobility, and vascular health.

Final Flush Factor Plus Review: Is Flush Factor Plus Worth Trying for Legs, Joints, and Heart Health?

After reviewing the clinical rationale, natural composition, and verified user feedback, Flush Factor Plus emerges as a top-tier solution for leg swelling, joint discomfort, and fluid retention issues.

Key takeaways:

  • Natural, Clinically-Inspired Formula: Bromelain from pineapple, asparagus extracts, beetroot, and hibiscus work synergistically to reduce inflammation, support fluid balance, and enhance vascular dilation.

  • AVP Regulation & Fluid Management: The formula targets arginine vasopressin balance, supporting natural reduction of leg and foot swelling, a common source of discomfort in millions of adults.

  • Circulation & Heart Health Support: Ingredients like beetroot and hibiscus improve blood flow, nutrient delivery, and heart wellness, creating a holistic approach beyond temporary symptom relief.

  • Proven Consumer Results: Over 30,000 verified reviews report significant reductions in leg pain, improved joint mobility, and better overall comfort, reflecting the formula's efficacy.

  • Safe, Natural, and Risk-Free: Mild, transient effects are rare, and every purchase is backed by a 60-day satisfaction guarantee, allowing confident testing.

For anyone struggling with persistent leg swelling, joint stiffness, or circulation concerns, Flush Factor Plus represents a breakthrough, evidence-backed supplement. It combines scientific mechanisms with verified user success, offering a trusted path to relief and mobility support.

>> Ready To See If Flush Factor Plus Can Make A Difference For You? Try It Today From The Official Website With Full 60-Day Protection-Risk-Free.

data.title: ROSEN, TRUSTED INVESTOR COUNSEL, Encourages Alto Neuroscience, Inc. Investors to Secure Counsel Before Important Deadline in Securities Class Action - ANRO
data.description:
NEW YORK, Aug. 16, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) --

WHY: Rosen Law Firm, a global investor rights law firm, reminds purchasers of common stock of Alto Neuroscience, Inc. (NYSE: ANRO): (i) pursuant and/or traceable to Alto’s initial public offering conducted on or about February 2, 2024 (the “IPO”); and/or (ii) securities between February 2, 2024 and October 22, 2024, both dates inclusive (the “Class Period”), of the important September 19, 2025 lead plaintiff deadline.

SO WHAT: If you purchased Alto securities you may be entitled to compensation without payment of any out of pocket fees or costs through a contingency fee arrangement.

WHAT TO DO NEXT: To join the Alto class action, go to https://rosenlegal.com/submit-form/?case_id=42321 or call Phillip Kim, Esq. at 866-767-3653 or email case@rosenlegal.com for more information. A class action lawsuit has already been filed. If you wish to serve as lead plaintiff, you must move the Court no later than September 19, 2025. A lead plaintiff is a representative party acting on behalf of other class members in directing the litigation.

WHY ROSEN LAW: We encourage investors to select qualified counsel with a track record of success in leadership roles. Often, firms issuing notices do not have comparable experience, resources, or any meaningful peer recognition. Many of these firms do not actually litigate securities class actions, but are merely middlemen that refer clients or partner with law firms that actually litigate the cases. Be wise in selecting counsel. The Rosen Law Firm represents investors throughout the globe, concentrating its practice in securities class actions and shareholder derivative litigation. Rosen Law Firm achieved the largest ever securities class action settlement against a Chinese Company at the time. Rosen Law Firm was Ranked No. 1 by ISS Securities Class Action Services for number of securities class action settlements in 2017. The firm has been ranked in the top 4 each year since 2013 and has recovered hundreds of millions of dollars for investors. In 2019 alone the firm secured over $438 million for investors. In 2020, founding partner Laurence Rosen was named by law360 as a Titan of Plaintiffs’ Bar. Many of the firm’s attorneys have been recognized by Lawdragon and Super Lawyers.

DETAILS OF THE CASE: According to the lawsuit, throughout the Class Period, defendants made materially false and misleading statements regarding Alto’s business, operations, and prospects. Specifically, the offering documents and defendants made false and/or misleading statements and/or failed to disclose that: (1) ALTO-100 was less effective in treating major depressive disorder (“MDD”) than defendants had led investors to believe; (2) accordingly, ALTO-100’s clinical, regulatory, and commercial prospects were overstated; (3) as a result, Alto’s business and/or financial prospects were overstated; and (4) as a result, Alto’s public statements were materially false and misleading at all relevant times. When the true details entered the market, the lawsuit claims that investors suffered damages.
To join the Alto class action, go to https://rosenlegal.com/submit-form/?case_id=42321 or call Phillip Kim, Esq. at 866-767-3653 or email case@rosenlegal.com for more information.

No Class Has Been Certified. Until a class is certified, you are not represented by counsel unless you retain one. You may select counsel of your choice. You may also remain an absent class member and do nothing at this point. An investor’s ability to share in any potential future recovery is not dependent upon serving as lead plaintiff.

Follow us for updates on LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/the-rosen-law-firm, on Twitter: https://twitter.com/rosen_firm or on Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/rosenlawfirm/.

Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee a similar outcome.

Contact Information:

        Laurence Rosen, Esq.
        Phillip Kim, Esq.
        The Rosen Law Firm, P.A.
        275 Madison Avenue, 40th Floor
        New York, NY 10016
        Tel: (212) 686-1060
        Toll Free: (866) 767-3653
        Fax: (212) 202-3827
        case@rosenlegal.com
        www.rosenlegal.com

data.title: SAREPTA LAWSUIT ALERT: Bragar Eagel & Squire, P.C. Reminds Investors that a Class Action Lawsuit Has Been Filed Against Sarepta Therapeutics, Inc. and Encourages Investors to Contact the Firm Before the August 25th Deadline
data.description:
NEW YORK, Aug. 16, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Bragar Eagel & Squire, P.C., a nationally recognized stockholder rights law firm, announces that a class action lawsuit has been filed against Sarepta Therapeutics, Inc. (“Sarepta” or the “Company”) (NASDAQ:SRPT) in the United States District Court for the Southern District of New York on behalf of all persons and entities who purchased or otherwise acquired Sarepta securities between June 22,2023 and June 24, 2025, both dates inclusive (the “Class Period”). Investors have until August 25, 2025 to apply to the Court to be appointed as lead plaintiff in the lawsuit.

Click here to participate in the action.

The complaint alleges that biopharmaceutical company Sarepta was engaged in the development of ELEVIDYS, a gene therapy intended to treat patients with Duchenne muscular dystrophy. Throughout the Class Period, Defendants made materially false and misleading statements that conditioned investors to believe ELEVIDYS was a safe therapy that could be expanded for wider application approval. Defendants also misled investors concerning ELEVIDYS's revenue outlook. Defendants positioned ELEVIDYS as having no hindrances to broader use, which would in turn allow for a strong growth in prescriptions. Defendants are alleged to have failed to disclose material adverse facts about the Company's compliance, operations, and outlook. Specifically, Defendants made false and/or misleading statements and/or failed to disclose that: (i) ELEVIDYS posed significant safety risks to patients; (ii) ELEVIDYS trial regimes and protocols failed to detect severe side effects; (iii) the severity of adverse events from ELEVIDYS treatment would cause the Company to halt recruitment and dosing in ELEVIDYS trials, attract regulatory scrutiny, and create greater risk around the therapy's present and expanded approvals; and (iv) as a result of the foregoing, Defendants materially misled with, and/or lacked a reasonable basis for, their positive statements.

On March 18, 2025, Sarepta issued a safety update on ELEVIDYS announcing that a patient had died following treatment with ELEVIDYS. On this news, Sarepta's stock price fell $27.81 per share, or 27.44%, to close at $73.54 per share on March 18, 2025. Next, on April 4, 2025, Sarepta disclosed that European Union member country authorities had requested that the independent data monitoring committee meet to review death announced on March 18, 2025. Sarepta simultaneously halted recruitment and dosing in some of the ELEVIDYS clinical studies. On this news, Sarepta's stock price fell $4.18 per share, or 7.13%, to close at $54.43 per share on April 4, 2025. Then, on June 15, 2025, Sarepta disclosed a second patient had died of acute liver failure following treatment with ELEVIDYS. The Company announced it was suspending shipments of ELEVIDYS for non-ambulatory patients while Sarepta took time to evaluate trial regimens and discussed findings with regulatory authorities. Sarepta also revealed that it was pausing dosing in one of its ELEVIDYS clinical studies. On this news, Sarepta's stock price fell $15.24 per share, or 42.12%, to close at $20.91 per share on June 15, 2025. Finally, on June 24, 2025, the United States Food and Drug Administration ("FDA") issued a Safety Communication announcing it had received reports of two deaths and was investigating the risk of acute liver failure with serious outcomes following treatment with ELEVIDYS. On this news, Sarepta's stock price fell $1.52 per share, or 8.01%, to close at $17.46 per share on June 25, 2025.

If you purchased or otherwise acquired Sarepta shares and suffered a loss, are a long-term stockholder, have information, would like to learn more about these claims, or have any questions concerning this announcement or your rights or interests with respect to these matters, please contact Brandon Walker or Marion Passmore by email at investigations@bespc.com, telephone at (212) 355-4648, or by filling out this contact form. There is no cost or obligation to you.

data.title: The rich already know how private equity mints money - and it's not from a 401(k)
data.description:
By Morey Stettner

You'll soon be able to invest your retirement savings in private equity. But should you?

The ultrawealthy are envied for many reasons. For instance, we wish we could access the same private-market investments that they favor.

Now, after the White House issued an executive order on Aug. 7, you may be able to invest like the billionaires do.

But would you want to?

The executive order allows ordinary retirement savers to invest in private assets and cryptocurrency. This will expand investment options for anyone with a 401(k) or similar tax-advantaged retirement plan.

It is a big deal - opening part of America's $12.4 trillion defined-contribution market to private-asset managers. The largest private-equity firms and other asset managers are salivating at the opportunity to pitch this untapped market of retirement savers.

Private assets encompass a range of investments that do not trade on a public exchange. Examples include hedge funds, private equity, private credit and infrastructure.

The case for private assets is they can provide a buffer against inflation - plus steady returns. The downsides include high fees, illiquidity and complexity.

The nation's biggest asset managers welcome the executive order. They want to develop funds that make private assets easier for people to buy, and argue that the added diversification serves savers' best interests.

Larry Fink, chief executive of BlackRock (BLK), says retirement savers should replace the traditional 60% stocks/40% bonds asset-allocation model with a 50/30/20 split: 50% stocks, 30% bonds and 20% private assets.

Read: Larry Fink proposes an alternative to the 60/40 portfolio. It means more fees.

Menu options

Should you be excited about this widening menu of investment choices? It depends on whom you ask.

Some investment professionals like the idea of making private assets more available to more people.

"Historically, a number of private-market strategies have produced higher performance and additional diversification in defined-benefit pensions," says Peter von Lehe, head of investment solutions and strategy at Neuberger Berman. "It's appropriate that a broader range of investors have access to private assets in their defined-contribution plans because of the potential for return and diversification that these long-term investments can provide."

However, von Lehe cautions that these investments are illiquid and "have a higher degree of complexity." He says his "most appropriate use case" for private-market investments is through professionally managed target-date funds or other funds that allocate a percentage of defined-contribution money to these complex but potentially more lucrative alternatives.

Read: Here's something the rich know about managing investment risk that can help you, too

Financial advisers have differing views on the role of private assets in client portfolios. Steven Roge, a certified financial planner in Bohemia, N.Y., says private markets are not for everyone.

"It's for people in the wealth-accumulation phase, say 40 to 50 years old, who have a long time horizon and a high risk tolerance," Roge says. "And they have to be sophisticated enough to understand it. We know if they don't understand it, they may not stick with it."

Of the firm's 300 clients, he says that "only about a dozen" fit the bill for adding private-market assets to their retirement accounts.

Even with the expanded investment options that may result from the White House's action, Roge remains a fan of passive strategies for most investors. "Indexing is how they will win over the long run," he says. "But some clients want something that's special and different" as they seek market-beating returns.

Given the illiquidity of private assets, Roge anticipates setting expectations for those clients who tend to monitor their portfolio daily - and who engage in frequent trading. "These private investments may only price four times a year," Roge says. "That's not enough action for certain clients who track their portfolio like a hawk."

In his personal portfolio, Roge uses private markets - especially private equity - to diversify his holdings. He says he allocates about 25% to alternative assets. "It helps me sleep at night knowing my portfolio isn't being pushed around by the volatility of public markets," he says.

Roge adds that he is not concerned about the current high valuations of private-equity funds. "The valuations [of private-equity funds] are more realistic than the erratic valuations we see in public markets on a daily basis," he says.

Other advisers are more skeptical of the White House executive order.

"It's less being done out of interest for the general public and more for private industry lobbying the [Trump] administration," says Alex Ruda, an adviser in Silver Spring, Md.

The executive order undoubtedly pleases asset managers and private-equity firms. For years, they've wanted to attract retirement savers' money. These savers bear primary responsibility for managing their 401(k) compared with today's older retirees, many of whom receive employer-funded defined-benefit pensions. While some younger savers enjoy picking their investments, others dread it.

"The average American worker isn't equipped to navigate these complex [private-market] investments," Ruda says. "And they may fall prey to a little performance chasing given where we are in the market cycle" - as private markets have outperformed publicly traded stocks since 2000.

Ruda feels so strongly about not incorporating private assets into client portfolios that he's willing to forgo newcomers who express such interest.

"If I wanted to broaden my client base, I'd have to play to what they want," he says. "But I don't have to do that. So I'd say to them, 'I'm not the best fit.'"

Read next: Here's what it's like to invest in private equity - and why you don't want it in your 401(k)

More: As private equity enters retirement plans, is it too dangerous for average investors to jump in?

-Morey Stettner

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  08-16-25 1151ET


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: REDDIT URGENT DEADLINE ALERT: Bragar Eagel & Squire, P.C. Reminds Reddit Investors of the August 18th Deadline for the Class Action Suit
data.description:
Bragar Eagel & Squire, P.C. Litigation Partner Brandon Walker Encourages Investors Who Suffered Losses In Reddit (RDDT) To Contact Him Directly To Discuss Their Options

If you purchased or acquired stock in Reddit between March 17, 2022 to September 22, 2022 and would like to discuss your legal rights, call Bragar Eagel & Squire partner Brandon Walker or Marion Passmore directly at (212) 355-4648.

NEW YORK, Aug. 16, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Bragar Eagel & Squire, P.C., a nationally recognized stockholder rights law firm, announces that a class action lawsuit has been filed against Reddit, Inc. (“Reddit” or the “Company”) (NYSE:RDDT) in the United States District Court for the Northern District of California on behalf of all persons and entities who purchased or otherwise acquired Reddit securities between October 29, 2024 and May 20, 2025, both dates inclusive (the “Class Period”). Investors have until August 18, 2025 to apply to the Court to be appointed as lead plaintiff in the lawsuit.

Click here to participate in the action.

The complaint alleges that the social media website Reddit receives a significant portion of its user traffic from Google Search, and Reddit’s primary source of revenue is generated from advertising to users on its own platform. Throughout the relevant period, Google Search began implementing new Artificial Intelligence (“AI”) capabilities such as AI Overview that could change the nature of search results.

Defendants are alleged to have made false and misleading statements, and failed to disclose, that: (i) changes in Google Search’s algorithm and features like AI Overview were causing users to stop their query on Google Search; (ii) these algorithm changes were materially different than prior instances of reduced traffic to the Reddit website; (iii) Defendants were aware that the increase in the query term “Reddit” on search engines was because users were getting the sought after answer from Google Search without having to go to Reddit, and not because they intended to visit Reddit; (iv) this zero-click search reality was dramatically reducing traffic to Reddit in a manner the Company was unable to overcome in the short term; and (v) Defendants, therefore, lacked a reasonable basis for its outlook on user rates and advertising revenues.

On May 1, 2025, after the market close, Reddit issued an earnings release announcing its first quarter 2024 financial results for the period ending March 31, 2025. The earnings release revealed that Reddit had experienced three consecutive quarters of deceleration in daily active user growth.

On this news, the price of Reddit’s common stock fell $4.96 per share, or 4.2%, from a closing price of $118.79 per share on May 1, 2025, to a closing price of $113.83 per share on May 2, 2025.

Then, on May 19, 2025, Wells Fargo analysts downgraded Reddit’s stock and lowered their price target to $115 per share from a previous $168 per share. Wells Fargo called Google Search’s implementation of new AI features as likely “permanent” disruptions on user traffic for Reddit.

On this news, the price of Reddit’s common stock fell $5.24 per share, or 4.6%, from a closing price of $113.23 per share on May 16, 2025, to a closing price of $107.99 per share on May 19, 2025.

Finally, on May 21, 2025, Baird analysts substantially downgraded Reddit’s stock, reducing the price target to $120 per share from the previous $140 per share. Citing similar concerns as the Wells Fargo analysts, Baird analysts additionally noted the new and disruptive developments in Google Search that had just been presented at the Google I/O developer conference.

On this news, the price of Reddit’s common stock fell $9.79 per share, or 9.3%, from a closing price of $105.64 per share on May 20, 2025, to a closing price of $95.85 per share on May 21, 2025.

If you purchased or otherwise acquired Reddit shares and suffered a loss, are a long-term stockholder, have information, would like to learn more about these claims, or have any questions concerning this announcement or your rights or interests with respect to these matters, please contact Brandon Walker or Marion Passmore by email at investigations@bespc.com, telephone at (212) 355-4648, or by filling out this contact form. There is no cost or obligation to you.

data.title: HIMS LAWSUIT ALERT: Bragar Eagel & Squire, P.C. Reminds Investors of Hims That A Lawsuit Has Been Filed and Urges Investors to Contact The Firm Before the August 25th Deadline
data.description:
Bragar Eagel & Squire, P.C. Litigation Partners Brandon Walker and Marion Passmore Encourage Investors Who Suffered Losses In Hims To Contact Them To Discuss Their Options

If you purchased or acquired securities in Hims between April 29, 2025 and June 23, 2025 and would like to discuss your legal rights, call Bragar Eagel & Squire partner Brandon Walker or Marion Passmore directly at (212) 355-4648.

NEW YORK, Aug. 16, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Bragar Eagel & Squire, P.C., a nationally recognized stockholder rights law firm, announces that a class action lawsuit has been filed against Hims & Hers Health, Inc. (“Hims & Hers” or the “Company”) (NYSE:HIMS) in the United States District Court for the Northern District of California on behalf of all persons and entities who purchased or otherwise acquired Hims securities between April 29, 2025 and June 23, 2025, both dates inclusive (the “Class Period”). Investors have until August 25, 2025 to apply to the Court to be appointed as lead plaintiff in the lawsuit.

Click here to participate in the action.

The complaint alleges that throughout the Class Period, Defendants made materially false and/or misleading statements, as well as failed to disclose material adverse facts about the Company's business, operations, and prospects. Specifically, the Complaint alleges that Defendants failed to disclose to investors: (1) that Hims was engaged in the "deceptive promotion and selling of illegitimate, knockoff versions of Wegovy® that put patient safety at risk;" (2) that, as a result, there was a substantial risk that the Company's collaboration with Novo Nordisk would be terminated; and (3) that, as a result of the foregoing, Defendants' positive statements about the Company's business, operations, and prospects were materially misleading and/or lacked a reasonable basis.

Additionally, the Complaint alleges that Defendants made false and/or misleading statements, as well as failed to disclose material facts, including that: (1) the communication between Hims and the pharmaceutical company Novo Nordisk A/S ("Novo") would facilitate a long-term collaboration that would ensure continued access to the weight-loss drug Wegovy for Hims subscribers; (2) Novo approved of Hims' offerings of compounded semaglutide products under the "personalization" exception; (3) branded Wegovy would be offered alongside compounded semaglutide options on the Hims platform, thereby expanding user choice; and (4) Defendants made positive statements abou the Novo partnership and Hims users' ongoing access to Wegovy alongside compounded semaglutide products.

data.title: Here's what it's like to invest in private equity - and why you don't want it in your 401(k)
data.description:
By Beth Pinsker

It's not just about risk and fees, but about knowing how much you have and having access to your money when you need it

Having private-equity investments in your 401(k) might not end up being quite what you were expecting.

For every public company in the U.S. whose stock you can buy directly or through mutual funds or exchange-traded funds, there might be 25 or more private companies that are also seeking investment. These range from major operations like Elon Musk's SpaceX to family-owned factories. There are no public filings for these companies, few analyst reports and generally little in the way of hard data to develop a real valuation of these entities.

This is what the financial world calls private equity and what the federal government now wants to allow workplace retirement funds to invest in through 401(k) plans, with details yet to be determined. Would individual investors be allowed to buy in through their 401(k) plans, perhaps at lower minimums than regulators now demand for accredited investors - currently those who have more than $1 million in capital at their disposal or make more than $200,000 in income annually. Would the investments take a similar shape to sector funds and be a category to invest in like international bonds or small-cap stocks? Would only portfolio managers have access, with the investment just one part of a larger slate inside a target-date or other balanced fund?

"I don't think most investors know what's in the funds they invest in today. I don't think they'll have a chance of understanding the private market," said Matthew Chancey, a certified financial planner who specializes in private-equity investments and is the author of a 2024 book titled "Tax Alpha Solutions."

Chancey believes that the opportunities in 401(k) plans will eventually take the form of "semi-liquid" funds that will keep a portion of their investments in some form that's both easy to redeem and steady, like Treasury bonds, and the rest in various private-equity investments. This would solve the biggest problem he sees with offering private-equity investments in 401(k)s, which is the redemption factor.

"Sometimes you have no liquidity options, and with some you can apply for redemption. You need to understand the options," he said. If an investor needs to force liquidity by selling out early, they might have to accept a cut in profit. They might also be refused.

This, of course, runs contrary to how most people interact with their retirement accounts. The majority of investors pick a single target-date fund for their whole 401(k) balance and stick with it, according to Vanguard's latest "How America Saves" report. That kind of fund is attached to a proposed retirement date. But what if, say, the 2050 fund contained private equity that was not yet ready to redeem in 2050, when the investors intended to retire? They'd expect to be able to start withdrawing money and be stymied if they couldn't.

"Liquidity is a structural challenge, and these strategies are often long-term and illiquid, which can be a mismatch for some retirement-plan participants," said Michael Cochran, a financial planner based in Fort Worth, Texas.

This is a scenario that Chancey has faced himself with private-equity investments. He put money into a private chain of hotels shortly before the COVID-19 pandemic and was expecting a payout.

"Did we lose those hotels? No, but it took years for those hotels to generate cash flows," he said. "We had no income from those investments for an extended period of time, but we worked it out."

The key to that, as always with investing, was diversification. He had enough money coming in from other investments to cover his needs while he waited.

Also read: The rich already know how private equity mints money - and it's not from a 401(k)

How to track private-equity investments

Another problem with private equity is how to track the investments. Today, 401(k) plans mostly favor mutual funds instead of ETFs or stocks because the back-end systems have a hard time tracking intraday trading. At the end of each day, the plan custodian is able to post a number that indicates a person's actual account value. Private equity is a big swing in the other direction - the reporting takes place monthly or quarterly, or sometimes even less often, and even then the number is a bit of a guess.

"There's a fundamental mismatch between the value data and frequency of reporting," said Philitsa Hanson, head of product for equity and fund administration for AllVue, which provides software solutions for private markets. She is currently working on technology solutions that would allow private-equity investments to be tracked inside of 401(k) systems.

"The 401(k) investor has an expectation that they read the news and stocks went up or down, or housing prices increased, and they can see the world around them and how that affects their portfolio," she said. "But not so much with private equity. Companies will fail for various reasons, and there's no cause and effect."

For financial adviser Marcos Segrera, the problem is really behavioral. "Happiness equals reality minus expectations," he said. "The expectation being set for 401(k) participants is that they are finally getting access to an exclusive, high-growth asset class that has enriched the ultrawealthy. They will be anticipating returns that significantly outperform the public markets. The reality is that after accounting for the higher fees, the long lockup periods and the confusing, smoothed-out performance statements, the net result may be far less impressive."

Down the road, Segrera expects that investors who opt into private equity will have regrets and think they would have been better off in a simple S&P 500 SPX index fund.

Even Chancey's clients, who come to him specifically in order to invest in alternative investments, have much of their wealth in traditional investment vehicles, so that mitigates any disappointments that might happen. They all have substantial assets already, and the money in private equity is for speculative growth. "I tell my clients, go get a financial adviser, and an insurance agent too. I'm a portion of their money, maybe 5%, maybe more sometimes," he said. "I had one client who had $50 million in normal investments and $150 million in illiquid private equity. It's a different deal."

If this doesn't feel like you, don't worry that you're being rushed into anything risky. This is still a ramp-up phase, and all the government's actions have done so far is unlock the door.

"It feels like there's a sense of inevitability, like everyone is getting ready, but I think from a time-scale perspective, it could hit the market in a year to 18 months, all contingent on regulatory changes needed," Hanson said. "The tech is close, but the operation has to change."

And of course, that's still only Step 1. There are regulations governing company retirement plans, and investors can always opt out of anything they consider risky in their plans.

"This could all be nothing, because nobody takes it up," said Hanson.

Got a question about investing, how it fits into your overall financial plan and what strategies can help you make the most out of your money? You can write to me at beth.pinsker@marketwatch.com. Please put "Fix My Portfolio" in the subject line.

You can also join the Retirement conversation in our Facebook community: Retire Better with MarketWatch.

By submitting your story to Dow Jones & Co., the publisher of MarketWatch, you understand and agree that we may use your story, or versions of it, in all media and platforms, including via third parties.

More Fix My Portfolio

-- Trump's order greenlights crypto in 401(k)s, so why isn't my job on board yet?

-- An obsession with avoiding the estate tax cost this top exec his marriage. Now his son is speaking out.

-- My relative, 80, was about to be scammed out of $40,000. Here's how I stopped it.

-Beth Pinsker

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  08-16-25 1121ET


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: The Powerball jackpot is now $565 million, but this man warns that playing the lottery can become an addiction
data.description:
By Charles Passy

Adam Osmond gambled away a fortune on lottery games, but eventually sought help

Adam Osmond lost about $1 million to an addiction playing lottery games. He was able to recover by channeling his energy into running, he says.

The Powerball jackpot has climbed to $565 million, with the next drawing set for Saturday night. It's far from the record jackpot territory of $2 billion, though it's still among the higher lottery prize amounts to date.

But for Adam Osmond, any jackpot fervor only serves as a reminder of the dark side of lotteries.

Osmond, a Connecticut resident, gave up buying lottery tickets more than a decade ago after losing around $1 million on various games, be they scratch-offs or drawings. He told MarketWatch that his addiction cost him his business (he owned two gas stations), his home and nearly any semblance of a normal life.

"When you talk about hitting bottom, I hit the worst of bottom," said Osmond, who sought treatment for his addiction. Today, he works as an accountant for Connecticut's housing department and runs marathons and other races in his free time.

Osmond's story may be an extreme one, but it's not entirely unique. Experts say that as state lotteries have surged in popularity - combined sales have grown from around $59 billion in 2010 to $113 billion in 2023 - so have the number of Americans who are addicted to playing the games.

The problem is only compounded when there's a big jackpot, experts add. Such events inevitably result in lots of news coverage and feed the idea that playing the lottery is a fun, safe pastime. And that's in spite of the fact the odds of winning the top Powerball prize is 1 in 292,201,338, meaning you have a greater chance of becoming a movie star or being killed by a bee sting.

As Americans are enticed to buy tickets with the possibility, however remote, of winning that life-changing sum, there's always the risk that some will become hooked on the lottery, say experts. And those who are already addicted may find themselves further incentivized to play.

Overall, 2.5 million U.S. adults have a severe addiction to gambling, while another 5 to 8 million have a mild or moderate problem, according to the National Council on Problem Gambling .

Osmond's story speaks to how addiction can spiral out of control. Born in Somalia, Osmond moved to this country more than 35 years ago and attended college. He began playing the lottery sporadically - "A dollar here or there," he said - but when he went into the gas-station business, he was able to buy tickets quickly and easily at the stores he owned, and his problems started.

Overall, 2.5 million U.S. adults have a severe addiction to gambling, while another 5 to 8 million have a mild or moderate problem

Osmond notes that he hit it big on more than one occasion, but that only fed his addiction. At some point around 15 years ago, he won a $50,000 prize on a drawing game. "I put the whole thing back" into more tickets, Osmond recalled. Naturally, he lost.

Osmond credits running as being what saved him from gambling - it served as a way for him to channel his energy positively, he said. He has competed in more than 500 races and is currently training for the Hartford Marathon in October. He is also the co-founder of the Run 169 Towns Society, which he says is the largest running group in Connecticut.

Perhaps the most troubling aspect of lotteries, Osmond said, is their popularity among the poor - meaning the people who can least afford to gamble. It's a point that has been cited in much research, with one study noting that those who earn less than $10,000 annually spend $597 (or about 6% of their income) on the lottery.

Ultimately, Osmond and others fear the problem won't go away easily, particularly because states are introducing more ways to play the lottery. Osmond considers himself fortunate in being able to break the cycle of his lottery addiction. And it's very much a cycle, he said: "Anytime you lose, you want to keep playing to get your money back."

Also on MarketWatch: Inflation alarm bells went off again and prices are rising. Just how bad is it going to get?

-Charles Passy

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  08-16-25 1127ET


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: ROSEN, TRUSTED INVESTOR COUNSEL, Encourages CTO Realty Growth, Inc. Investors to Secure Counsel Before Important Deadline in Securities Class Action – CTO, CTO-PA
data.description:
NEW YORK, Aug. 16, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) --

WHY: Rosen Law Firm, a global investor rights law firm, announces the filing of a class action lawsuit on behalf of purchasers of securities of CTO Realty Growth, Inc. (NYSE: CTO, CTO-PA) between February 18, 2021 and June 24, 2025, both dates inclusive (the “Class Period”). A class action lawsuit has already been filed. If you wish to serve as lead plaintiff, you must move the Court no later than October 7, 2025.

SO WHAT: If you purchased CTO Realty securities during the Class Period you may be entitled to compensation without payment of any out of pocket fees or costs through a contingency fee arrangement.

WHAT TO DO NEXT: To join the CTO Realty class action, go to https://rosenlegal.com/submit-form/?case_id=43344 or call Phillip Kim, Esq. at 866-767-3653 or email case@rosenlegal.com for more information. A class action lawsuit has already been filed. If you wish to serve as lead plaintiff, you must move the Court no later than October 7, 2025. A lead plaintiff is a representative party acting on behalf of other class members in directing the litigation.

WHY ROSEN LAW: We encourage investors to select qualified counsel with a track record of success in leadership roles. Often, firms issuing notices do not have comparable experience, resources, or any meaningful peer recognition. Be wise in selecting counsel. The Rosen Law Firm represents investors throughout the globe, concentrating its practice in securities class actions and shareholder derivative litigation. Rosen Law Firm achieved the largest ever securities class action settlement against a Chinese Company at the time. Rosen Law Firm was Ranked No. 1 by ISS Securities Class Action Services for number of securities class action settlements in 2017. The firm has been ranked in the top 4 each year since 2013 and has recovered hundreds of millions of dollars for investors. In 2019 alone the firm secured over $438 million for investors. In 2020, founding partner Laurence Rosen was named by law360 as a Titan of Plaintiffs’ Bar. Many of the firm’s attorneys have been recognized by Lawdragon and Super Lawyers.

DETAILS OF THE CASE: According to the lawsuit, throughout the Class Period, defendants made false and misleading statements and/or failed to disclose that: (1) CTO’s dividends were less sustainable than defendants had led investors to believe; (2) CTO Realty Growth used deceptive and unsustainable practices to artificially inflate its Adjusted Funds from Operations (“AFFO”) and overstate the true profitability of its Ashford Lane property; (3) accordingly, CTO Realty Growth’s business and/or financial prospects were overstated; and (4) as a result, defendants’ public statements were materially false and misleading at all relevant times. When the true details entered the market, the lawsuit claims that investors suffered damages.

To join the CTO Realty class action, go to https://rosenlegal.com/submit-form/?case_id=43344 or call Phillip Kim, Esq. at 866-767-3653 or email case@rosenlegal.com for more information.

No Class Has Been Certified. Until a class is certified, you are not represented by counsel unless you retain one. You may select counsel of your choice. You may also remain an absent class member and do nothing at this point. An investor’s ability to share in any potential future recovery is not dependent upon serving as lead plaintiff.

Follow us for updates on LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/the-rosen-law-firm, on Twitter: https://twitter.com/rosen_firm or on Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/rosenlawfirm/.

Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee a similar outcome.

data.title: Nexa International brands Unveils Royal Oasis in Pererenan, Asia’s Top 3 Neighborhood
data.description:
JAKARTA, Indonesia, Aug. 16, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Pererenan Ranks Among Asia’s Top 3 Neighborhoods — Nexa International brands Unveils Royal Oasis Collection at Its Heart

Named one of Asia’s top three neighborhoods by TimeOut Worldwide, Pererenan is quickly becoming a global favorite — known for its seamless blend of raw natural beauty, creative spirit and sophisticated coastal lifestyle.

In the middle of this thriving, ever-evolving area, Nexa International brands presents Royal Oasis Collection — a boutique residential project that brings together clean, thoughtful architecture, private rooftop gardens and a design philosophy deeply in tune with the soul of the place.

Slated for completion within a year, Royal Oasis Collection isn’t just a peaceful sanctuary — it’s a smart investment. With high-end property demand in Pererenan on the rise, early buyers can look forward to strong value growth and long-term rewards.

Royal Oasis Collection by Nexa international brands — modern tropical living, reimagined with quiet elegance and solid returns on the horizon.


There are places you visit — and there are places that transform you.

Set amid the lush hills of Ubud, Tampak Siring Resort is poised to become one such destination.
This visionary new project is the result of a creative partnership between Nexa International brands and Andra Matin, the iconic Indonesian architect known for his poetic take on tropical minimalism. The collaboration merges refined design, cultural sensitivity and architectural soul — all rooted in the timeless rhythm of Bali’s most spiritual landscape.
More than just a resort, Tampak Siring is a statement: about what it means to build with intention, to honor the land and to create spaces that make you pause, be in the exact moment and offers quiet luxury.

For Nexa International brands international group and Andra Matin, this partnership marks a meeting of visionaries — and a mutual elevation of identity. It also signals a rare investment opportunity, with projected growth as the resort nears completion and interest in Ubud’s design-forward hospitality market continues to rise.

With its seamless blend of natural materials, sculpted light, and immersive stillness, Tampak Siring Resort offers more than a stay — it offers a state of mind.
Coming soon.
